description: Admin UI for tripod-backend - manage users, organizations, projects, languages, apps, and roles with a global map view

userStories.title: Init Vite project with React 18, TypeScript, and Tailwind v4
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the base project scaffolding so that we can start building the console.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite project created with React 18 and TypeScript
Tailwind CSS v4 configured with @tailwindcss/vite plugin

All dependencies installed: react-router, zustand, axios, @radix-ui/react-dialog, @radix-ui/react-select, @radix-ui/react-tabs, @radix-ui/react-tooltip, @radix-ui/react-popover, @radix-ui/react-label, lucide-react, sonner, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge
vite.config.ts includes API proxy from /api to http://localhost:8000
tsconfig.json configured with @/ path alias for src/
Project builds without errors (npm run build)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stack: React 18, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Zustand, Axios, Radix UI, lucide-react, sonner. Use @vitejs/plugin-react and @tailwindcss/vite.

userStories.title: Add Shema design tokens, cn utility, and centralized style constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the design system foundation so that all components use consistent tokens and styles.
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.css has Tailwind v4 @theme block with all Shema tokens (branco, areia, azul, telha, verde-claro, verde, preto, surface, surface-alt) for both light and dark modes
Fonts imported: Montserrat (UI) and Merriweather (content)
src/utils/cn.ts exports cn() function using clsx + tailwind-merge
src/styles/cards.ts exports card style constants (base, hover, interactive, padded)
src/styles/badges.ts exports badge style constants (success, pending, error, active, inactive)
src/styles/layout.ts exports layout style constants (page, container, grid, sidebar, main)
src/styles/states.ts exports state style constants (empty, loading, error, warning)
src/styles/index.ts re-exports all style modules
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Colors: branco=#F6F5EB, areia=#C5C29F, azul=#89AAA3, telha=#BE4A01, verde-claro=#777D45, verde=#3F3E20, preto=#0A0703, surface=#FFFFFF, surface-alt=#F3F2EB. Fonts: Montserrat (UI) + Merriweather (content).

userStories.title: Create UI primitives (shadcn/Radix-based components)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable UI primitives so that pages use consistent interactive elements.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ui/button.tsx - Button with variants (default/destructive/outline/ghost) and sizes using cva
src/components/ui/input.tsx - Input with Shema focus styles (ring-telha, border-telha)
src/components/ui/label.tsx - Label component using Radix Label
src/components/ui/badge.tsx - Badge with variants using cva
src/components/ui/card.tsx - Card, CardHeader, CardContent, CardFooter with Shema card styles
src/components/ui/dialog.tsx - Dialog using Radix Dialog primitive with overlay and content
src/components/ui/select.tsx - Select using Radix Select primitive
src/components/ui/tabs.tsx - Tabs using Radix Tabs primitive
src/components/ui/textarea.tsx - Textarea with Shema focus styles
src/components/ui/tooltip.tsx - Tooltip using Radix Tooltip primitive
src/components/ui/popover.tsx - Popover using Radix Popover primitive
All components use cn() for class merging and accept className prop
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow shadcn/ui pattern: Radix primitive + Tailwind + cva + cn(). Use Shema design tokens for all colors. Primary button uses telha. Focus rings use telha. Borders use areia.

userStories.title: Set up ThemeContext, React Router routing, and AppShell layout
userStories.description: As a user, I need the app shell with sidebar navigation so I can navigate between sections.
acceptanceCriteria: src/contexts/ThemeContext.tsx provides light/dark/system theme with localStorage persistence
React Router v7 configured in App.tsx with all routes from the routing table in CLAUDE.md
src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx wraps protected routes with sidebar + header + Outlet
src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx with collapsible navigation (w-64 expanded, w-16 collapsed)
src/components/layout/AppHeader.tsx with theme toggle (sun/moon icon) and app title

Sidebar shows navigation items with lucide-react icons: My Apps (LayoutGrid), Languages (Languages), Organizations (Building2), Projects (FolderOpen), Map (Globe), Users (Users) [admin only], Manage Apps (AppWindow) [admin only]
Active route highlighted with telha accent in sidebar
Mobile sidebar as overlay with dark backdrop
Root path / redirects to /app/dashboard
/app index redirects to /app/dashboard
Placeholder page components for all routes (renders route title)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Admin items (Users, Manage Apps) will be conditionally rendered later when AuthContext exists. For now, always show them. Sidebar has user profile / logout area at bottom.

userStories.title: Build common components and onboardingStore
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared common components and onboarding state management for use across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/common/LoadingSpinner.tsx - centered spinner component with configurable size
src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx - accepts icon (LucideIcon), title, description, actionLabel?, onAction?
src/components/common/ConfirmDialog.tsx - confirmation modal with cancel/confirm using Dialog primitive
src/components/common/ErrorBoundary.tsx - React error boundary with fallback UI
src/components/common/InfoTooltip.tsx - (i) icon (lucide Info) in text-verde/40 at 14-16px with Radix Tooltip showing content string
src/components/common/FeatureSpotlight.tsx - wraps children with pulsing ring + Radix Popover with title, description, 'Got it' dismiss button
src/stores/onboardingStore.ts - Zustand store with: dismissedSpotlights string[], dismiss(key), isDismissed(key), resetAll()
onboardingStore persisted to localStorage key tc_onboarding
FeatureSpotlight uses onboardingStore to check/set dismissed state
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FeatureSpotlight props: featureKey, title, description, side (top/bottom/left/right), children. Max 1 spotlight visible per page. InfoTooltip appears to the right of its label with ml-1.

userStories.title: Create API client skeleton and TypeScript type definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API client and type definitions so that pages can make backend calls with proper typing.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/api.ts exports a single Axios instance with baseURL /api
Namespaced API exports: authAPI, usersAPI, appsAPI, languagesAPI, orgsAPI, projectsAPI, rolesAPI
authAPI: login, logout, refresh, me, myRoles
usersAPI: list, get, update, listRoles
appsAPI: list, myApps, create, get, update, listRoles
languagesAPI: list, create, get, getByCode
orgsAPI: list, create, get, update, listMembers, addMember, removeMember
projectsAPI: list, create, get, update, updateLocation, listUserAccess, listOrgAccess, grantUser, grantOrg, revokeUser, revokeOrg
rolesAPI: assign, revoke, check
src/types/ directory with type files: auth.ts, user.ts, app.ts, language.ts, organization.ts, project.ts, role.ts, index.ts
src/constants/app.ts exports token keys (tc_access_token, tc_refresh_token)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key distinction: appsAPI.myApps() -> GET /api/apps/my-apps (all users), appsAPI.list() -> GET /api/apps (admin only). Types should match backend Pydantic schemas.

userStories.title: Implement AuthContext with JWT auth flow and Axios interceptors
userStories.description: As a user, I need authentication so I can log in and have my session maintained automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx provides: user, isPlatformAdmin, appRoles, isLoading, login, logout, isAppAdmin
login(email, password) calls POST /api/auth/login, stores access_token and refresh_token in localStorage
On mount, checks localStorage for tokens and calls GET /api/auth/me + GET /api/auth/my-roles to restore session
logout() calls POST /api/auth/logout, clears tokens from localStorage, resets user state
isPlatformAdmin derived from user.is_platform_admin
isAppAdmin(appKey) returns true if appRoles contains {app_key: appKey, role_key: 'admin'}
Axios request interceptor attaches Authorization: Bearer header from localStorage token
Axios response interceptor handles 401: calls POST /api/auth/refresh with refresh_token, updates both tokens, retries original request
On refresh failure, clears tokens and redirects to /login
AuthProvider wraps the app in App.tsx
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Token localStorage keys: tc_access_token, tc_refresh_token. Auth endpoints: POST /api/auth/login -> AuthResponse, POST /api/auth/refresh -> TokenResponse, POST /api/auth/logout (204), GET /api/auth/me -> UserResponse, GET /api/auth/my-roles -> MyRoleResponse[].

userStories.title: Build LoginPage with route protection and error pages
userStories.description: As a user, I need a login page and proper route protection so unauthenticated users are redirected.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/pages/LoginPage.tsx with email + password form using Shema design tokens
Login form has email input, password input, and submit button (telha primary)
Form calls AuthContext.login(), shows error toast via sonner on failure
Successful login redirects to /app/dashboard
AppShell checks AuthContext user state - redirects to /login if user is null and not loading
Admin routes (/app/users, /app/apps) check isPlatformAdmin - render AccessDeniedPage if false
Sidebar hides Users and Manage Apps items when isPlatformAdmin is false
src/components/pages/AccessDeniedPage.tsx with 'Unauthorized' message and link to dashboard
src/components/pages/NotFoundPage.tsx with 404 message and link to dashboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Login page: centered card on branco background. Use Montserrat font, telha primary button, areia borders.

userStories.title: Add App model columns and Alembic migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need additional columns on the App model to support the app hub feature.
acceptanceCriteria: App model in /workspace/tripod-backend/app/db/models/auth.py updated with new nullable columns: description (Text), icon_url (String), app_url (String), ios_url (String), android_url (String), platform (String, default='web')
New Alembic migration file created in /workspace/tripod-backend/alembic/versions/ that adds the 6 new columns to the apps table
Migration has both upgrade and downgrade functions
Downgrade drops the 6 new columns
All new columns are nullable or have defaults so existing data is not broken
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Working directory: /workspace/tripod-backend/. Read existing App model in app/db/models/auth.py first. Read existing migrations in alembic/versions/ for pattern. Columns: description (Text, nullable), icon_url (String, nullable), app_url (String, nullable), ios_url (String, nullable), android_url (String, nullable), platform (String, default='web').

userStories.title: Add Users API (service, schemas, router)
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I need user management endpoints so the console can list and manage users.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/user/__init__.py created with functions: list_users(db), get_user_by_id(db, user_id) raising NotFoundError, update_user(db, user_id, is_active?, is_platform_admin?) raising NotFoundError, list_user_roles(db, user_id)

app/models/user.py created with Pydantic schemas: UserListResponse (id, email, display_name, is_active, is_platform_admin, created_at), UserUpdate (is_active?: bool, is_platform_admin?: bool), UserRoleResponse (app_key, role_key, granted_at)
app/api/users.py router created with: GET /api/users (list all, admin only), GET /api/users/{user_id}, PATCH /api/users/{user_id} (admin only), GET /api/users/{user_id}/roles
All endpoints require authentication via get_current_user dependency
GET /api/users and PATCH restricted to platform admin (check is_platform_admin)
Router registered in app/main.py with prefix /api/users and tag 'users'
http/users.http created with example requests for all 4 endpoints
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Working directory: /workspace/tripod-backend/. Read existing services (app/services/auth/, app/services/project/) and routers (app/api/auth.py) for patterns. Read app/core/exceptions.py for exception types. User model already exists in app/db/models/auth.py. Never import HTTPException in services.

userStories.title: Add Users service unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the users service to ensure correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_user_service.py created
Test list_users returns all users ordered appropriately
Test get_user_by_id returns correct user
Test get_user_by_id raises NotFoundError with match string for non-existent user ID
Test update_user toggles is_active from true to false
Test update_user toggles is_platform_admin from false to true
Test update_user raises NotFoundError for non-existent user ID
Test list_user_roles returns correct roles for user with roles
Test list_user_roles returns empty list for user with no roles
All tests use make_* factories from tests/baker.py
All tests use @pytest.mark.asyncio decorator and -> None return type
All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/test_user_service.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Working directory: /workspace/tripod-backend/. Follow patterns from tests/test_language_service.py and tests/test_authorization_service.py. Import service as module: from app.services import user_service (or from app.services.user import ...). Use make_user, make_app, make_role, make_user_app_role from tests.baker.

userStories.title: Add Apps API (service, schemas, router)
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I need app management endpoints. All users need a my-apps endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/app/__init__.py created with functions: list_apps(db), create_app(db, app_key, name, ...) raising ConflictError on duplicate app_key, get_app_or_404(db, app_id) raising NotFoundError, update_app(db, app_id, ...) raising NotFoundError, list_user_apps(db, user_id) returning apps with user's role keys, list_app_roles(db, app_id)

app/models/app.py created with Pydantic schemas: AppCreate (app_key, name, description?, icon_url?, app_url?, ios_url?, android_url?, platform?, is_active?), AppUpdate (name?, description?, icon_url?, app_url?, ios_url?, android_url?, platform?, is_active?), AppResponse (all fields including new columns), UserAppResponse (app fields + roles list[str])

app/api/apps.py router created with: GET /api/apps (admin only), GET /api/apps/my-apps (any authenticated user), POST /api/apps (admin only), GET /api/apps/{app_id}, PATCH /api/apps/{app_id} (admin only), GET /api/apps/{app_id}/roles
GET /api/apps/my-apps returns apps the current user has access to based on UserAppRole, with their role_keys
Router registered in app/main.py with prefix /api/apps and tag 'apps'
http/apps.http created with example requests for all 6 endpoints
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Working directory: /workspace/tripod-backend/. list_user_apps queries UserAppRole joined with App to find apps where user has roles, groups by app, collects role_keys. AppResponse must include new columns: description, icon_url, app_url, ios_url, android_url, platform.

userStories.title: Add Apps service unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the apps service to ensure correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_app_service.py created
Test list_apps returns all apps
Test create_app creates app with all fields including new columns
Test create_app raises ConflictError with match string on duplicate app_key
Test get_app_or_404 returns correct app
Test get_app_or_404 raises NotFoundError with match string for non-existent app
Test update_app updates name and description
Test update_app raises NotFoundError for non-existent app
Test list_user_apps returns only apps where user has roles, with correct role_keys
Test list_user_apps returns empty list when user has no app roles
Test list_app_roles returns roles for specified app
All tests use make_* factories from tests/baker.py
All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/test_app_service.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Working directory: /workspace/tripod-backend/. Use make_app, make_user, make_role, make_user_app_role factories. The db_session fixture seeds one default app (meaning-map-generator), so list_apps may return more than just test-created apps.

http/organizations.http updated with examples for the 3 new endpoints

Uses Shema card styles (bg-surface, border-areia/30, shadow-sm, rounded-lg)

userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want to view user details and manage their app roles.
acceptanceCriteria: UserDetailPage replaces placeholder, fetches user from usersAPI.get(userId) and roles from usersAPI.listRoles(userId)
User info card: email, display_name, is_active toggle (calls usersAPI.update), is_platform_admin toggle (calls usersAPI.update)
App roles table: columns App Key, Role Key, Granted At, Actions
Assign Role button opens Dialog: App select dropdown (from appsAPI.list()), Role select (admin/member), calls rolesAPI.assign()
Revoke button per role row opens ConfirmDialog, calls rolesAPI.revoke()
Success/error toasts for all mutations
Back navigation link to /app/users
Loading states for user and roles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Role assign body: {user_id, app_key, role_key}. Role revoke body: {user_id, app_key, role_key}. Platform admins see all apps in dropdown.

userStories.title: Build AppsPage (admin apps management)
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want to manage apps in the system.
acceptanceCriteria: AppsPage replaces placeholder, fetches from appsAPI.list()
HTML table with columns: App Key, Name, Platform, Active (badge), Actions
Create App button opens Dialog: app_key, name, description, platform select (web/mobile/both), icon_url, app_url, ios_url, android_url, is_active checkbox
Create calls appsAPI.create(), shows success toast, refreshes list
Edit button per row opens Dialog pre-filled, calls appsAPI.update()
409 conflict toast for duplicate app_key
Click row navigates to /app/apps/:appId
Page only accessible to platform admins
LoadingSpinner, guided EmptyState
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Admin-only page. Depends on US-012 for Apps API endpoints.

userStories.title: Build AppDetailPage with roles and metadata
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want to view app details and see its assigned roles.
acceptanceCriteria: AppDetailPage replaces placeholder, fetches app from appsAPI.get(appId) and roles from appsAPI.listRoles(appId)
App info card: app_key, name, description, platform badge, icon preview (img if icon_url set), app_url link, ios_url link, android_url link, is_active badge
Edit button opens Dialog to update app metadata fields
Roles table: columns Role Key, Label, Description, System (badge for is_system)
Back navigation link to /app/apps
Loading states for app and roles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Admin-only page. Depends on US-012 for GET /api/apps/{appId} and GET /api/apps/{appId}/roles.

userStories.title: Build MapPage with Leaflet world map and project markers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see project locations on a world map.
acceptanceCriteria: react-leaflet, leaflet, and @types/leaflet packages installed
MapPage replaces placeholder, fetches projects from projectsAPI.list()
Filters projects to those with both latitude and longitude set
Leaflet map rendered with OpenStreetMap tiles, fills content area
One marker per project at (latitude, longitude)
Marker popup shows: project name, location_display_name (or 'No location name'), link to /app/projects/:projectId
Leaflet CSS properly imported so tiles and marker icons render correctly
Guided empty state when no projects have coordinates ('No project locations to show')
FeatureSpotlight on map container for first visit (featureKey: 'map-first-visit')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Dependencies: react-leaflet, leaflet, @types/leaflet. Marker clustering (react-leaflet-cluster) is optional enhancement. Import leaflet CSS in index.css or MapPage.

userStories.title: Add contextual guidance across all pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want contextual help throughout the app so I can understand features without a tutorial.
acceptanceCriteria: InfoTooltip added to all page section headers with descriptive text explaining the section purpose
InfoTooltip on non-obvious table columns: Slug, Code, App Key, Platform Admin, Platform, Active
InfoTooltip on form fields with specific requirements (Code field, Slug field, App Key field)
FeatureSpotlight on Project Access tab (featureKey: 'project-access-first-visit')
FeatureSpotlight on Apps admin page (featureKey: 'apps-admin-first-visit')
All spotlights dismiss correctly via 'Got it' button and persist via onboardingStore
Maximum 1 spotlight visible per page at a time
All empty states have educational guidance text (not just 'No data found')
No tutorial walls or modal blockers - all guidance non-intrusive
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Review all pages systematically. Spotlight keys: map-first-visit (already added in US-030), project-access-first-visit, apps-admin-first-visit. Keep tooltip text under 2 sentences.
